message:
feat(tui): add interactive widget inspector overlay for TUI debugging

Implement a full widget inspector overlay that enables developers and
power users to explore the TUI's widget tree interactively, inspect
properties, and understand the layout hierarchy at runtime.

Core additions:
- InspectorOverlayModel struct holding InspectorState, ordered
  widget list (Vec<HitId>), and widget-to-rect mapping for spatial
  navigation
- New OverlayLayer::Inspector variant (z=8, above Help) that traps
  focus and captures navigation + dismiss keybindings
- Inspector overlay rendering in tui_chrome.rs: draws a translucent
  panel listing all widgets with their HitId, type, bounding rect,
  and selected-state highlighting; selected widgets get a visible
  border highlight in the main view

Keybinding integration (tui_keymap.rs):
- Add Ctrl+Shift+I toggle binding to open/close the inspector
- j/k navigation within the inspector widget list
- Esc to dismiss the inspector overlay
- Enter to select/focus a widget for detailed property inspection

Overlay system updates:
- OverlayLayer::traps_focus() now includes Inspector in the
  focus-trapping set, ensuring keyboard events are correctly
  routed when the inspector is active
- Import ftui_render::frame::HitId and ftui_widgets::inspector
  types (InspectorState, WidgetInfo) for the new overlay model

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
